call_summary: The customer had a repeat quality issue with a study table and wanted to exchange it for a sturdier version. However, the agent was unable to handle the complaint effectively, lacked product knowledge to suggest alternatives, and failed to show empathy or offer a resolution that would have retained the customer. The call ended partially resolved, with a high risk of churn as the customer mentioned considering a different option.

The customer was looking to visit the local Gurgaon store to check the availability of the Alice Executive Table. The agent was able to provide the nearest store location, offer a callback from the local team, and mention an additional in-store discount. While the agent couldn't provide the exact dimensions or color options, they were able to address the customer's primary need and set up a follow-up interaction to assist with the in-store visit.

A customer called WoodenStreet and was greeted by agent Ayodhya. The customer requested to be transferred to the installation department. The agent acknowledged the request and informed the customer they were being transferred to the support team.

The customer was interested in visiting a Wooden Street store in Bangalore to see the furniture in person before placing an order. The agent provided clear information about the available store locations and offered to share the address details. The call was handled well, with the customer seeming ready to visit the store. The agent could have made a stronger closing attempt to book a store visit and suggest any in-store discounts or promotions to create a sense of urgency.

A customer from Jaipur called WoodenStreet inquiring about customized beds with modified designs and dimensions. The agent informed the customer that WoodenStreet does not customize bed designs or dimensions, though upholstery changes are possible at an additional charge. The call ended with the agent offering to connect via WhatsApp for future furniture needs.

A customer called WoodenStreet regarding a dressing table delivered 2-3 days ago in Gurgaon that still required installation. The agent acknowledged the request and informed the customer they would be connected to the installation team. The call was very brief with minimal interaction captured in the transcript.

The customer called back in response to a missed call from WoodenStreet. The agent identified the customer as Swapol Kumar Chatterjee and confirmed that the call was related to an existing order. The agent attempted to transfer the call to the relevant team handling the order.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about lamination services for her existing dining table. The agent informed her that WoodenStreet does not offer lamination services and only deals in furniture. The call ended briefly with a polite closing and an open invitation for future furniture needs.

A customer from Ahmed Nagar near Pune called WoodenStreet inquiring about a Chowpal-style wooden balcony swing. The agent informed the customer about nearby Pune stores with a display unit in Punawale, explained the made-to-order manufacturing process with approximately one month delivery time, and shared details about shisham wood material, 3-year warranty, delivery charges, and self-installation requirement. The customer ended the call saying they would visit the store and revert.

High-value bulk order inquiry from US-based customer setting up hotel in Andhra Pradesh, requiring 41+ furniture items (18 beds, 5 side tables, 11 TV units, 7 wardrobes) with expectation of significant bulk discount. Agent successfully identified opportunity, documented all items, and committed to prepare comprehensive quotation with delivery/installation details via WhatsApp follow-up within customer's timezone (8-11 AM IST). Customer remained engaged and satisfied throughout, with no churn risk. However, agent missed critical upsell opportunities (mattresses, assembly labor packages, hotel decor packages) and struggled with product name clarifications, resulting in extended call time. Agent also failed to confirm exact delivery timeline or build urgency around hotel opening date, which are critical for sales pipeline management. Call outcome is positive with good closing technique, but revenue opportunity was partially left on table.

A customer from Chennai named Muni Kumar called WoodenStreet inquiring about a wall unit that can accommodate a 65-inch TV. Agent Ayushi acknowledged the requirement, offered to share product designs via WhatsApp, and mentioned the availability of Chennai stores in Anna Nagar and Palekar. The agent collected the customer's name and pin code and promised to share options along with arranging a video call if needed.

A customer from Kalyanagar called to inquire about bean bags and beds available at the nearby WoodenStreet experience store. The agent informed the customer that bean bags are not on display but can be ordered online with 20-25 day delivery, and shared information about queen-size beds starting at ₹11,999. The agent shared product links, store details, and collected the customer's name and PIN code before closing.

A customer called WoodenStreet to track the status of their order and expressed frustration about receiving false commitments. The agent (Zahid) briefly acknowledged the concern but then transferred the call to the support team without adequately addressing the customer's issue. The call ended with the customer being placed on hold for a transfer.

A customer called WoodenStreet's head office number inquiring about crockery cabinets and sideboards available for display at the Golden Street outlet before planning a store visit. The agent (Ayushi) provided information about display models available in-store, store timings, ongoing Diwali sale, and delivery timelines. The customer decided to visit the store independently without scheduling a formal callback.

A customer from Kota, Rajasthan called WoodenStreet inquiring about multiple furniture items including sofas, dining tables, office desks, and chairs. The agent identified the nearest store in Gandhi Nagar City Mall, Kota, and confirmed the customer's plan to visit in person. The agent also offered to share product listings via WhatsApp to help the customer shortlist items before the store visit.

Customer Khushboo Dubey called to inquire about the status of her order. The agent, Mayank, failed to gather any order details and immediately attempted to transfer the call to the delivery team without attempting to assist the customer directly. The call ended abruptly without any resolution or confirmation of transfer.

A customer named Dina called WoodenStreet to confirm her product delivery date, stating she needs the product before 6th October. The call landed in the sales department, and the agent transferred her to the delivery team without resolving her query. The interaction was brief and ended with a transfer.

Sanjay Kothari, a customer from Mumbai, called WoodenStreet to furnish a large flat requiring multiple items including queen beds with storage, solid wood wardrobes, an ironing table, crockery unit, and a glass-top dining table. The agent captured his requirements, informed him about the Thane store, and promised to send store details and arrange a callback from a specialist. The call ended with a follow-up plan including a WhatsApp share of the flat layout for space planning.

A customer called to inquire about the largest WoodenStreet store in Bangalore where she could view sofas, dining tables, and related furniture. The agent struggled with pin code verification but eventually recommended the HSR Layout store as the best option. The agent also briefly mentioned an ongoing website discount and warranty before closing the call.

A customer named Kajal from Kaskana Furniture called WoodenStreet's centralized number inquiring about the Ranchi store location on Radium Road. The agent confirmed the store details and offered to arrange a callback from the local store. The customer agreed and provided her name and PIN code (829108) for the callback arrangement.

Customer Sachin called WoodenStreet to inquire about purchasing 6 dining chairs from a store near Gokapet, Hyderabad. The agent provided store location details, pricing information (starting at ₹4,500 per chair, totaling ₹25,194 for six), and mentioned an HDFC credit card discount. The call ended without a clear conversion or follow-up commitment.

A customer named Komal called WoodenStreet to inquire about a store that was undergoing a location shift, as she wanted to visit it. The agent informed her that the shifting was not yet complete and offered her the option to visit the Juhu store in the meantime. The agent collected her name and pincode and promised a callback once the new store location was operational.

Customer Rakesh called WoodenStreet inquiring about a Shisham wood queen bed with storage, looking for pricing and offers. Agent Mayank provided product details, ongoing Diwali sale discounts, and delivery timelines. The customer expressed a budget constraint of around 25,000 rupees and said he would think and call back, with the agent following up via WhatsApp with designs.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about L-shaped sectional sofas, specifically about delivery timelines for online vs. in-store orders for a Bangalore address (pin 560062). The agent explained the warehouse-based delivery system, checked available stock options, and ultimately suggested a store visit to the nearest Jayanagar/Tanakpur Road Bangalore outlet. The customer agreed to visit the store the same day to check quality and finalize a purchase.

The customer was interested in purchasing a room partition product but the agent was unable to provide specific details about the dimensions, availability, and installation of the product. This led to customer frustration, especially since the customer had a time constraint. The agent could not resolve the issue within the given timeframe, resulting in a partially resolved call with a medium risk of the customer churning.

A customer called WoodenStreet to follow up on a delivery status confusion — they received a message saying their order was ready for delivery, but a previous executive had told them it would take three more weeks. The agent (named Zahid, though the transcript labels are swapped) offered to connect the customer to the support team without fully resolving the confusion. The call ended without a clear resolution or confirmed next steps.

A customer named Chetan from Bangalore called to inquire about office chairs available for display at the JP Nagar (Jainagar) WoodenStreet store. The agent confirmed multiple office chair options are on display, mentioned festive season discounts, and provided delivery timelines of 3-4 days (local warehouse) or 15-20 days (national warehouse). The agent offered to connect the customer with the store team for more detailed assistance.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring whether the company offers repair services for two broken chairs made of the same material. The agent informed the customer that WoodenStreet only takes orders for furniture listed on their official website and does not offer repair services for external products. The call ended quickly with no further engagement or exploration of the customer's needs.

A customer called WoodenStreet's centralized number to inquire about a Calibria Shisham wood 2-seater dining set he found on the website, wanting to visit a store to feel the product before purchasing. The agent checked store inventory, found the 2-seater was not available for display but offered alternatives, and discovered the product may need to be made on order. The agent promised to call back within 10-15 minutes with delivery confirmation after consulting the production team.

A customer called regarding a cancelled refund and expressed frustration. The agent, who appeared to be in a reversed role (acting as customer initially), checked the refund record and placed the customer on hold to transfer the call to the relevant team. The call ended without resolution as it was escalated.

A customer called WoodenStreet wanting to sell their used wooden bed (D1 model, 74x84 size with storage). The agent clarified that WoodenStreet does not offer a furniture buyback or resale service. The call ended quickly with no further engagement or attempt to redirect the customer toward a purchase.

A customer called WoodenStreet looking for a carpenter who could drill legs into resin coffee table tops she makes herself. The agent confirmed that WoodenStreet does not offer carpentry services and has no referrals for such professionals. The agent briefly mentioned WoodenStreet sells dressing tables but could not assist with the customer's specific need.

Shabbir, a customer based in Bangalore traveling to Surat, called WoodenStreet inquiring about furniture for his Surat home, including two beds, two wardrobes with lofts, a study table, a TV unit, and a three-seater sofa. The agent provided store timing, warranty details, customization limitations, and offered to send product images and a quotation via WhatsApp. The call ended with the customer agreeing to share his detailed requirements over WhatsApp for a personalized quote.

A customer from South Kolkata called WoodenStreet to find the nearest experience center and inquire about L-shaped sofa combat availability and pricing. The agent identified the Jadavpur and Salt Lake stores, provided pricing information (initially quoting ₹38,000 then correcting to ₹48,000), and confirmed that the Salt Lake store has an L-shaped sofa combat on display. The call ended with the agent promising to share real-time product images and store details over WhatsApp, and offering to connect the customer with the local store team.

A customer called WoodenStreet to find the nearest store location and inquire about the Della Session Sofa with armrest. The agent provided store details in Andheri East, clarified that the specific sofa is not available for display but offered a callback from the store team and mentioned additional discounts. The call ended with a follow-up callback being arranged for the customer.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about custom furniture services, including design, dimension, and finish customizations. The agent (Ayushi) informed the customer that customizations are not available for standard products, only potentially for bulk/fabric orders. The call ended without any resolution or next steps offered to the customer.

A customer from Lakhimpur Khiri, UP called WoodenStreet inquiring about office counters and customized furniture. The agent informed him that the nearest stores are in Lucknow, Ghaziabad, and Noida, and that WoodenStreet does not offer office counters or customized furniture. The call ended without a sale or follow-up scheduled.

A customer called WoodenStreet to inquire whether the Ranchi store was currently open. The agent confirmed the store hours (10 AM to 10 PM) and offered to arrange a callback from the store team for reconfirmation. The call was brief and informational with a positive resolution.

The call appears to be an inbound call where the customer (Sachin) identified themselves as SST and the agent provided an ID number (1044242). The agent then transferred the call to the support team without further engagement. The conversation was extremely brief and incomplete, with no substantive interaction recorded.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about rental furniture after seeing an advertisement. The agent clarified that WoodenStreet does not offer rental furniture and only deals in freshly made products. The call ended briefly after the clarification with a generic closing.

Customer Roshan called regarding a chair order placed on 28th September that is not reflecting in his app/account, despite payment being debited from his account with a payment confirmation received. The agent acknowledged the issue, identified a previous order from 25th September but confirmed it was a different order, and decided to transfer the call to the support team without resolving it directly.

A customer called WoodenStreet's centralized number to inquire about TV cabinets for a 55-inch TV and sectional fabric sofas before visiting a nearby store. The agent provided product range, material comparisons, warranty details, and store timing information. The call ended with the agent collecting the customer's name and pin code to share store details and product links via WhatsApp.

Arvind from Jodhpur called after online research and colleague referral to verify two products — Albus 3-seater fabric sofa (iris cream) and Tahira TV unit (frosty white) — before ordering. Since WoodenStreet has no Jodhpur store, he wanted to visit the manufacturing unit/warehouse to physically inspect products. Agent successfully addressed his delivery timeline concerns, offered real-time customer images instead of professional photos, confirmed local discount eligibility, provided manufacturing head contact, and arranged warehouse visit (pending date confirmation). Customer ended the call satisfied and hinted at potential additional purchases during visit. However, the agent missed locking in a concrete warehouse visit appointment, provided contradictory product availability information, and failed to capitalize on upsell signals by pre-sharing curated product recommendations for his potential additional budget.

A customer from Kolkata's Park Street area (PIN 700016) called WoodenStreet to inquire about a three-door wardrobe and the nearest store location. The agent confirmed a Park Street store, provided a starting price of ₹13,789 for three-door wardrobes, shared a WhatsApp catalogue, and informed the customer about store timings and delivery timelines. The call ended with the agent directing the customer to visit the store for an additional 10% discount.

Customer Dr. Abhilasha called to inquire about WoodenStreet store locations in Chandigarh and asked about the availability of bunk beds and master bedroom beds. The agent confirmed stores in Sector 17A and Mohali, informed her that bunk beds are not on display but can be ordered online, and checked serviceability for her Himachal Pradesh PIN code (177001). A callback was arranged to confirm delivery feasibility and potential delivery charges.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about a 4-door wooden chest/wardrobe after waiting 2-3 days for a callback that she claims never came. The agent acknowledged the missed contacts and offered to arrange a video call demo from the Greater Noida store where the product is available. The call ended with the agent promising a callback with a video demo arranged.

A customer called regarding a late order placed in September, which was promised to be dispatched by February 3rd. The agent (Sachin) acknowledged the delay and offered to connect the customer to the support team for resolution. The call was transferred without full resolution on the agent's end.

A frustrated customer called WoodenStreet for the third time regarding a payment issue where money was debited from his account on September 20th but no order was generated. The agent had limited system access and could only see one existing order, and ultimately transferred the call to the support team. The customer had already received payment confirmation emails from both his bank and WoodenStreet but the order was never created.

Customer Abhishek called to inquire about the availability of small wooden mandirs (home temples) at WoodenStreet's Saganagar branch. The agent confirmed approximately six home temple options are available in the store and provided delivery timelines ranging from 3-4 days to 4-5 weeks depending on stock. The customer decided to visit the store to check quality and options in person.

A customer called WoodenStreet to inquire about their bookshelf order (ID: 10433610), likely regarding delivery or order status. The agent, identifying themselves as part of the sales team, offered to transfer the call to the delivery team. The call ended with a transfer being initiated without any resolution confirmed.

Customer called from East Delhi (PIN 110092) to confirm nearest WoodenStreet store location and inquire about a durable 4-seater dining table with chairs for her rented home. She had previously purchased from WoodenStreet but experienced loosening of joints and wanted a sturdier option. Agent provided confusing store location information (listed multiple Noida/Delhi stores without clear proximity guidance) and muddled EMI/pricing explanation (0% vs 16% interest, pricing quotes ranged from Rs 13,799 to Rs 24,000 without clear hierarchy). However, agent successfully identified product needs, explained sheesham wood warranty benefit comprehensively at the call's end, and scheduled store team callback from Gaur City location. Customer ended with cautious interest, planning to visit store in 2-3 days but lacking clarity on best option. Biggest missed opportunity: agent never leveraged customer's positive past experience to build loyalty, offer referral discount, or position sheesham wood as the durability upgrade she was explicitly seeking.

A customer named Raj called WoodenStreet inquiring about a physical store in Andheri West, looking to purchase four single beds, a dining table, and household items. Agent Kapish confirmed the Lakshmi Lane store location, shared product availability details, material options, delivery timelines, and ongoing Diwali sale discounts. The call ended with the customer agreeing to visit the store before making a purchase decision.

A customer called WoodenStreet to confirm if the Khagaul Road store was open and to check store hours. The agent confirmed the store is open all 7 days from 10 AM to 9:30 PM. The call was brief and informational with no product discussion.

A customer called inquiring about the Edmond Mango Wood White Cabinet after seeing it online, and asked about pricing and availability at the Phoenix Mall Lucknow store. The customer also expressed interest in finding a lower-priced console option for their showroom. The agent provided pricing details, mentioned an in-store discount, and offered to share options via WhatsApp while encouraging a store visit.

A customer called WoodenStreet's head office looking for pricing on sofas and dining tables, having expected to reach the Sector 17 store. The agent provided detailed pricing for 5-seater sofas (L-shape and 3+1+1 configurations) and 6-seater dining sets, collected the customer's pin code, and invited them to visit the store. The call ended with the agent mentioning in-store discounts and wishing the customer a good day.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about a 5-seater sofa set (3+1+1 or 3+2 configuration) in both wooden and fabric options, along with pricing and EMI payment options. The agent provided basic pricing and EMI information but could not fully address the partial payment query, so escalated to a Mohali store callback. The customer requested an immediate callback and ended the call without a firm commitment.

A customer named Shruti called WoodenStreet inquiring about room partitions for display at the Mysore store. The agent (Vishal) checked and confirmed no partitions were available for in-store display in Mysore, but offered to share catalog options via WhatsApp. The agent also informed the customer about free delivery and installation available for the Mysore location.

A customer named Tushar called WoodenStreet to inquire about adding two Samantha reversible stools to an existing order and wanted them customized in the same dark green olive velvet fabric as his already-placed Trace Lounge Chair order. The agent acknowledged the customization request but could not confirm availability on the spot, and instead arranged for a callback from the Noida store where Tushar's primary contact is based. The call ended with the agent advising the customer to either wait for the store callback or reach out to his contact directly.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about a product called 'Golden Ivory Chowki' seen on the website, wanting to know if it was available in Pune stores for same-day pickup. The agent checked and confirmed the product is not displayed in any store and is a third-party vendor item with a 12-15 day delivery timeline. The customer's urgent need could not be fulfilled and the call ended without a sale.

A customer called WoodenStreet regarding a delivery-related message they had received about one of their orders. The agent, who appeared to be confused about their role, attempted to transfer the customer to the support or delivery team. The call was extremely brief and did not resolve the customer's query.

A customer from Bangalore called WoodenStreet to inquire about which stores carry hydraulic beds, specifically in king and queen sizes. Agent Vishal identified the nearest store at JP Nagar, confirmed hydraulic bed availability, and offered to share catalogs and product details via WhatsApp. The call ended with the agent providing his direct contact number and encouraging a store visit.

The customer, Sachin, called WoodenStreet because he placed an order for a mattress worth ₹8000 the previous day, the payment was deducted from his bank account, but no order confirmation was received and the order was not reflecting in the system. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to the support team after a brief conversation. No resolution was provided directly by the agent.

A customer named Geeta from Banjo called WoodenStreet inquiring about a king-size wooden double cot with storage, for which she had already received a quotation of 13,000 from a store representative. The agent was unable to locate the previous interaction or the specific product, as the customer only had a picture but no product name. The agent arranged a callback from the Kannakpura store team to assist with the exact product and pricing.

A customer called WoodenStreet regarding a delivery expected the following day. The agent, who appeared to be confused about their role, transferred the call to the customer support team without addressing the query. The call was very brief and largely unresolved at the agent level.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about outdoor furniture and specifically wanted to visit a physical store in Whitefield, Bangalore to see and feel the products before purchasing. The agent checked with their team, confirmed outdoor furniture availability at the Whitefield store, and arranged a callback from the local store team. The call ended with the customer satisfied and awaiting the callback.

A customer called back after receiving a missed call from WoodenStreet's number. The agent assumed it was a delivery-related callback and redirected the customer to the delivery team without properly verifying the context. The call was extremely brief and resolved with minimal information exchange.

A customer named Anitha from Bangalore (PIN 560011) called inquiring about a memory foam mattress in size 78x66 inches for a 12-year-old girl, hoping for next-day delivery. The agent identified warehouse availability, explained WoodenStreet's Penguin brand mattress with a 10-year warranty, and promised to follow up via WhatsApp with catalog details and a callback from the Jayanagar store. The call ended with a follow-up action pending, no confirmed order.

A customer called inquiring about a computer/study table with bookshelf options and asked about a WoodenStreet store in Banerjee. The agent provided the store location and mentioned home delivery availability, but the conversation was disjointed and lacked clarity. The call ended without a confirmed purchase, visit, or follow-up commitment.

A customer called WoodenStreet's head office number looking for the Banjara Hills, Hyderabad store contact, as she was physically trying to locate the store but could not find it. The agent acknowledged the query and offered to arrange a callback from the Banjara Hills store. The call ended with the agent closing without confirming whether the callback was actually arranged or the customer's issue was resolved.

A customer named Shreya from Jolly Grant (Dehradun area) called to inquire about study tables available at WoodenStreet, asking about pricing and designs. The agent provided pricing information across different material categories and offered to send catalog images via WhatsApp. The call ended with the agent collecting the customer's name and PIN code to share designs.

A customer called WoodenStreet looking for a 7x7 white wardrobe that she couldn't find on the website, and also mentioned a previous online order of two shoe racks. The agent acknowledged her existing order and informed her that an executor had tried to reach her. The agent offered to connect her with the relevant executor on the same call.

A customer from Pune called WoodenStreet inquiring about a custom white wardrobe (7 feet wide) with specific internal fittings including drawers, shelves, and hanging rods, which she could not find on the website. The agent noted her requirements, offered to share designs via WhatsApp, and directed her to nearby Pune stores. The conversation also revealed the customer had already ordered two items from the website the previous day, and the agent discussed available discounts including HDFC credit card and store-based offers.

A customer calling from Chennai wanted to purchase a sofa-cum-bed and a coffee table, with billing at his Chennai address but delivery to his hometown in West Godavari (PIN 534-101). The agent clarified that delivery to that PIN code is possible but will attract an additional charge of approximately ₹14,000. The customer expressed surprise at the charge and ended the call saying he would think about it.

A customer calling from Chennai contacted WoodenStreet and faced a language barrier as the agent could only handle Hindi or English. The customer wanted to speak with someone from a local Chennai store, and the agent informed them of multiple Chennai store locations and promised a callback arrangement. The call ended with a callback being scheduled without any deeper needs discovery.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about custom-built furniture, specifically large storage cabinets and home temples, after seeing 'customization' mentioned on the website. The agent clarified the scope of customization (fabric only, no dimension/design changes), shared WhatsApp catalogue links, and arranged a callback from the Lucknow Goomti Nagar store. The call ended with the customer receiving relevant product links and a store callback being scheduled.

A caller named Jeevan from H-Tech Exhibitions contacted WoodenStreet's customer support line to connect with a marketing decision-maker regarding upcoming multi-city exhibitions in Bangalore, Mumbai, Delhi, and Hyderabad. The agent was unable to transfer the call directly and redirected the customer to email marketing@woodenstreet.com. The call ended with the customer acknowledging the email ID and agreeing to follow up via email.

A customer called WoodenStreet looking to check availability of a computer table that could be delivered quickly, and wanted to connect with the Prashant Vihar store specifically. The agent clarified that stores are experience centers only and that orders must be placed online or in-store (not from floor stock). The customer declined further assistance and ended the call.

A customer from Jaipur called WoodenStreet inquiring about purchasing a TV unit urgently, wanting delivery within 1-2 days. The agent informed the customer that items are not available for instant purchase and warehouse stock would take 10-12 days to deliver from Jodhpur. The call ended without a conversion as the customer's urgent timeline could not be met.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about kids chairs. The agent attempted to assist by sharing a WhatsApp link containing kids chair options, but the call suffered from poor audio quality and communication gaps. The conversation ended abruptly without a clear resolution or next step.

A customer called WoodenStreet to get the location of the Jaipur (Jainagarh) showroom, mentioning she had previously spoken to someone from the store. The agent assisted by sharing a WhatsApp message containing a Google Maps link to the showroom. The call was brief and transactional, focused solely on providing location information.

A customer called WoodenStreet inquiring about a foldable single bed with a metal frame, and was guided to a product from the brand 'Honey Touch' with dimensions of approximately 6 feet by 3 feet. The agent confirmed availability and informed the customer that delivery would take 2-3 weeks from a Jodhpur warehouse, ruling out any possibility of same-day store pickup.

A customer called WoodenStreet and was greeted by agent Ayushi. The customer requested to be redirected to the Marathalli store to inquire about the delivery date of an existing order. Ayushi acknowledged the request and proceeded to transfer the call.

A customer called to request that WoodenStreet stop sending repeated calls and WhatsApp messages from their chatbot/calling team, as their delivery had already been rescheduled to 2nd October. The agent acknowledged the DND request and attempted to transfer the customer to the support team for a delivery update. The call ended with a warm closing but no resolution on the delivery query.

A customer called WoodenStreet to confirm the exact location of the C-Scheme Jaipur store. The agent provided the location details (KK Tower, above Serra Style Studio, Prithviraj Market). The customer revealed they were already near the store and were asking for directional confirmation before visiting.

A customer called to track their order but was unable to do so on their own. The agent, who incorrectly identified themselves as being from 'Odin Street' instead of WoodenStreet, directed the customer to go online and connect with the customer support team. The call ended without resolving the customer's issue.

A customer called WoodenStreet's centralized number intending to speak with the Jaipur office about furniture in Jharkhand. The agent was confused about the customer's location and context, and the call ended abruptly after minimal information was gathered. The agent promised a callback from the Jharkhand store but the conversation was largely incoherent and unproductive.

A customer called WoodenStreet to inquire whether the Patna store was open and to confirm its location before visiting. The agent provided the store address on Khagol Road and confirmed operating hours as 10 AM to 10 PM. The customer confirmed intent to visit the store and declined further assistance.

A customer called WoodenStreet's corporate office in Udaipur looking for the contact number of the Annanagar, Chennai store manager, whom they had spoken with previously but lost the contact. The agent was unable to provide the direct number but offered to arrange a callback from the Annanagar store team within 5-10 minutes. The customer accepted the callback arrangement and the call ended cordially.

A customer called WoodenStreet to inquire about the store location in Vijay Nagar, Indore. The agent was unable to provide precise landmark-based directions and arranged a callback from the store instead. The call ended without the customer receiving a direct answer to their query.

A customer called WoodenStreet to follow up on a TV unit ordered on 23rd September that had not been delivered yet. The customer expressed significant frustration over lack of delivery updates and difficulty reaching support for the past 4 days. The agent apologized briefly and transferred the call to the delivery support team.

A customer from Brandli Nagar, Gujarat called WoodenStreet inquiring about furniture for a 2BHK apartment. The customer expressed interest in ready-made furniture and specifically asked about kitchen furniture. The agent identified the kitchen requirement as modular and arranged a callback from the modular kitchen team while offering to guide the customer on other furniture categories.

A customer from Pune called WoodenStreet inquiring about king-size hydraulic storage beds and their pricing. The agent provided product information, warranty details, and informed the customer about multiple Pune store locations. A callback was arranged from the Baner store since the customer was planning an immediate visit.

A customer from Hyderabad called WoodenStreet and was connected to the corporate office in Rajasthan instead of the local Nagol branch. The agent identified the customer's interest in buying cots and briefly mentioned available sizes before promising a callback from the Nagol branch within 5-10 minutes. The call ended with a callback arrangement rather than any further product discussion.

Customer from Delhi (Anandabhar, near Indrapuram) is looking for a 2-door wardrobe/cupboard with shelves, 6 feet height, for storage-only use. Agent attempted discovery but provided confusing dimension information and failed to confirm whether matching products are available in Delhi warehouse. Audio quality issues frustrated the interaction further. Agent pivoted to 3-door alternative (Cadence) without exhausting 2-door options or explaining lead times clearly. Customer deferred decision with 'I'll get back' — no booking, no follow-up scheduled, no catalog shared. High churn risk: agent missed opportunity to retain warm lead by providing clear product confirmation, booking a store visit, or scheduling specialist callback.

Customer Unnati Modi from Mumbai (Prabhadevi area, PIN 400767) called to inquire about the Alice Executive Table in Exotic Teak finish priced at ₹12,999. She needed urgent delivery within 2-3 days, but the agent found the item was only available at the national warehouse in Jodhpur with an estimated 3-4 week delivery timeline. The agent arranged a callback from the nearest store (Nadiri/Mumbai) to explore faster delivery options.

Customer Girish called WoodenStreet looking for a sofa set, study table for a kid, and a TV unit. Agent Tapesh from the Jaipur head office identified requirements, confirmed the nearest Bangalore store (Sarjapur), and shared product and delivery information. The call ended with the agent promising to send a WhatsApp filter link and store visit details.

A customer called WoodenStreet to confirm whether the Loudon Street showroom in Kolkata (PIN 700016) was open on a festival day. The agent confirmed store hours (10 AM to 9:30 PM, all seven days) and proactively arranged a callback from the store to give the customer added assurance.
